block_01: -24% & counting apparently, according to 3M Company Stocks.
block_02: On the 26th April, 3M opened in the red following a disappointing 1st Quarter Earnings Report. Shares had dived almost 8%, following the news that the company has missed expectations in regards to profits & sales. It was the company's biggest one-day plunge in over 30 years.
block_03: Adding more salt to the wound, 3M announced it would be slashing 2000 jobs (industrial, health-care & consumer products)
block_04: An analyst at J.P. Morgan has reiterated that the stock status is “underweight” (below average rating) a rating he had awarded 3M back in September 2017. He also added that the stock could drop a further 15% from current levels.
block_05: The company famous for scotch tape & post it notes has appeared to come unstuck.
